text: Four weeks of elliptical bicycle (EBIKE) training yielded similar physiological and performance outcomes compared with run-only training in experienced runners, with no significant group differences in VO2max, ventilatory threshold, respiratory compensation point, running economy, or 5,000m time trial performance.

Ventilatory threshold improved significantly after both EBIKE-only training and run-only training over 4 weeks, increasing from a baseline of 40.17 ml·kg·min to 41.60 and 42.33 ml·kg·min respectively.
Cross-training with a running-similar modality can maintain VO2max, running economy, and 5K performance over a 4-week period without any running.
Cross-training methods that mimic running biomechanics can reduce impact forces associated with running injuries while preserving running-specific fitness.
Short-term substitution of running with elliptical-based cross-training does not result in detraining of aerobic capacity or running performance in trained runners.
population: experienced runners (mean age 22, mean 9.25 years experience)
